While the focus remains largely on preemies below certain thresholds, the information suggests that any deviation from typical growth parameters increases vulnerability to ROP. Therefore, I concur with my earlier stance emphasizing the necessity for quick assessments by ophthalmologists to mitigate serious lifelong consequences stemming from unmanaged ROP. My agreement stands firm supported now more comprehensively by data linking small-for-gestational-age status directly to heightened ROP risk justifying proactive consultative measures promptly initiated.
